sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presents with neck pain that began after a car crash in February of this year.
He has experienced multiple car crashes, with the most recent one occurring when he was coming home from the pain clinic.
He rates his pain as a 12 out of 10, describing it as sharp and incapacitating.
He has been experiencing stiffness, sharp pain, and numbness in his left arm and right leg.
He also reports headaches, dizziness, and visual disturbances, but these symptoms are not recent.
He has been taking fentanyl, but has not received a prescription for several weeks.
He has been to therapy and a pain clinic, and has undergone surgery in the past.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue since the accident, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms in his body.
Current medications: Fentanyl (dose and frequency not specified).
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and the muscle.
He has a moderate range of movement in his neck, but it hurts to bend forward and backwards.
There is no bruising, swelling, or laceration present.
Test Results: An x-ray was performed, which did not show a fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain that can be explained by multiple factors, including the recent car crash.
Problem list: Neck sprain; active; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500 milligrams, to be taken 6 to 8 hours as needed, to help relax the muscles in the area and take off some of the tension and pain.
I instructed the patient to put ice on the affected area whenever he experiences bruising or swelling, and to perform exercises to improve range of motion.
I also referred the patient to physical therapy and will refer him to pain medication if the Robaxin is not effective.
I will refer him to an MRI to get a thorough image of the area.
I will give him a note to excuse him from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient after the MRI results are available to discuss further treatment and plan.
